title: Transportes e Direções
subtitle: Transport, Directions & Movement Prepositions
description: Learn transportation vocabulary, how to ask for and give directions, and movement prepositions (para, até, por).

vocabulary.word: o autocarro / o comboio / o metro
vocabulary.pronunciation: oo oh-toh-KAH-roo / oh kohm-BOY-oo / oh MEH-troh
vocabulary.example: Vim no autocarro n.º 24. O comboio vai para o Porto.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I came by bus no. 24. The train goes to Porto.

vocabulary.word: o avião / o barco / o táxi / a bicicleta
vocabulary.pronunciation: oo ah-vee-AH-nyoo / oh BAHR-koo / oh TAH-ksee / ah bee-see-KLEH-tah
vocabulary.example: Vamos de avião para Lisboa. Às vezes venho de bicicleta.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: We're going by plane to Lisbon. Sometimes I come by bicycle.
vocabulary.tip: Use 'de' for means of transport: de avião, de comboio, de metro, de bicicleta

vocabulary.word: ir a / ir para / ir até
vocabulary.translation: To go to (short trip) / To go to (destination) / To go to (as far as)
vocabulary.pronunciation: eer ah / eer PAH-rah / eer ah-TOO
vocabulary.example: Vou ao supermercado. Vou para Lisboa. Vou até à praça.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I'm going to the supermarket. I'm going to Lisbon. I'm going as far as the square.
vocabulary.tip: 'A' = short trip, 'para' = destination, 'até' = as far as (limit/boundary)

vocabulary.word: passar por / sair de / chegar a
vocabulary.translation: To pass through / To leave from / To arrive at
vocabulary.pronunciation: pah-SAR por / sa-EER de / cheh-GAR ah
vocabulary.example: Passa pelo metro. Saio de casa às 8h. Chego à universidade às 9h.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Go through the metro. I leave home at 8am. I arrive at university at 9am.
vocabulary.tip: 'Por' = through, 'de' = from, 'a' = at/to. These prepositions combine with articles.

vocabulary.translation: Where is...? / How do I get to...?
vocabulary.example: — Onde é a estação? — Como chego ao centro?
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: — Where is the station? — How do I get to the center?
vocabulary.tip: Use 'Onde é...?' for location. Use 'Como chego a...?' for directions to a place.

vocabulary.word: vire à esquerda / vire à direita / siga em frente
vocabulary.translation: Turn left / Turn right / Go straight ahead
vocabulary.pronunciation: VEER-ah ah eh-KEHR-dah / VEER-ah ah dee-RAY-tah / SEE-gah eem FREHN-teh
vocabulary.ipa: /ˈviɾɨ ɐ ˈɛʃkeɾdɐ/ / /ˈviɾɨ ɐ diˈɾɐjtɐ/ / /ˈsiɡɐ ɐ̃j ˈfɾẽtɨ/
vocabulary.example: Vire à esquerda, depois siga em frente.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Turn left, then go straight ahead.
vocabulary.tip: These use the formal imperative (vire, siga). Informal: 'vira', 'segue'

vocabulary.word: a esquina / o semáforo / a praça / a rua
vocabulary.translation: The corner / The traffic light / The square / The street
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah eh-SKEE-nah / oh seh-MAH-foh-roo / ah PRAH-sah / ah ROO-ah
vocabulary.example: Vire na esquina. Pare no semáforo. A praça fica na rua principal.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Turn at the corner. Stop at the traffic light. The square is on the main street.
vocabulary.tip: Key landmarks for giving and understanding directions in a city

vocabulary.word: perto / longe / ao lado / em frente
vocabulary.translation: Near / Far / Next to / In front of
vocabulary.pronunciation: PEHR-too / LON-zheh / oh LAH-doo / eem FREHN-teh
vocabulary.example: A estação é perto. O mercado fica ao lado do banco. O café é em frente da escola.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: The station is near. The market is next to the bank. The café is in front of the school.
vocabulary.tip: Combine with 'de' and articles: perto de, ao lado de, em frente de

vocabulary.translation: To catch / To catch the bus
vocabulary.example: Apanho o autocarro n.º 24 para a universidade.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I catch bus no. 24 to the university.
vocabulary.tip: 'Apanhar' = to catch (a bus/train). In Brazil: 'pegar'. Also means 'to pick up'

vocabulary.word: a pé / de carro / de metro
vocabulary.translation: On foot / By car / By metro
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah peh / deh KAH-roo / deh MEH-troh
vocabulary.example: Venho a pé. Posso vir de carro. Vamos de metro.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I come on foot. I can come by car. Let's go by metro.
vocabulary.tip: 'A pé' = on foot (no article). 'De + transport' = by transport: de carro, de metro, de autocarro

sections.heading: Means of Transport
sections.body: To talk about how you travel, use 'de' + means of transport: 'de autocarro' (by bus), 'de comboio' (by train), 'de metro' (by metro), 'de avião' (by plane), 'de bicicleta' (by bike), 'de carro' (by car), 'de táxi' (by taxi). For walking, use 'a pé' (on foot). To say you take a specific vehicle, use 'apanhar' (to catch): 'Apanho o autocarro n.º 24' (I take bus no. 24). To ask how someone travels: 'Como vens para a universidade?' (How do you come to university?).

sections.heading: Means of Transport
rows: ir de autocarro / apanhar o autocarro

sections.heading: Movement Prepositions: A, PARA, ATÉ, POR
sections.body: Portuguese has several prepositions for movement, each with a distinct meaning. 'A' indicates movement to a short-term destination where you'll return: 'Vou ao supermercado' (I'm going to the supermarket — and I'll come back). 'Para' indicates a longer-term destination or permanent move: 'Vou para Lisboa' (I'm going to Lisbon — to live/stay there). 'Até' means 'as far as' or 'up to' — it sets a limit: 'Vou até à praça' (I'm going as far as the square). 'Por' means 'through' or 'via': 'Passa pelo centro' (Go through the center). All of these combine with definite articles.

sections.heading: Movement Prepositions — A vs PARA vs ATÉ vs POR
Asking for and Giving Directions
sections.body: To ask where something is: 'Onde é...?' (Where is...?) or 'Onde fica...?' (Where is... located?). To ask how to get somewhere: 'Como chego a...?' (How do I get to...?) or 'Como se vai para...?' (How does one go to...?). For giving directions, use the formal imperative: 'Vire à esquerda' (Turn left), 'Siga em frente' (Go straight ahead), 'Passe pelo semáforo' (Pass through the traffic light), 'Vire na esquina' (Turn at the corner). Key landmarks: esquina (corner), semáforo (traffic light), praça (square), rua (street), cruzamento (crossroads).

items.translation: How do I get to the station?

items.portuguese: Siga em frente e vire à esquerda na esquina.
items.translation: Go straight ahead and turn left at the corner.
items.pronunciation: SEE-gah eem FREHN-teh ee VEER-ah ah eh-KEHR-dah nah eh-SKEE-nah.

items.portuguese: Apanho o autocarro n.º 24 para a universidade.
items.translation: I catch bus no. 24 to the university.
items.pronunciation: ah-PAHN-yoo oo oh-toh-KAH-roo NOH-meh-roo VEEN-tee kwah-troh pah-rah ah oo-nee-veh-see-DAH-deh.

items.portuguese: Passa pelo centro e depois vai até à praça.
items.translation: Go through the center and then go as far as the square.
items.pronunciation: PAH-sah peh-loo SEN-troo ee deh-POYSH vah-ee ah-TOO ah PRAH-sah.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes & Tips
items: Use 'de' for means of transport: 'ir de autocarro' (go by bus), NOT 'ir em autocarro'.
'A' vs 'Para': 'Vou ao supermercado' (short trip) vs 'Vou para Lisboa' (destination).
'Até' sets a limit/boundary: 'Vou até à praça' (I'll go as far as the square, no further).
'Por' means 'through/via': 'Passa pelo centro' (Go through the center).
For directions, use the formal imperative: 'Vire' (turn), 'Siga' (go/continue), 'Passe' (pass).
'A pé' = on foot (no article, no preposition). Don't say 'de pé' or 'em pé' for walking.
'Apanhar' = to catch (a bus/train). Very common in Portugal. In Brazil, use 'pegar'.
Prepositions combine with articles: a+o=ao, a+a=à, por+o=pelo, por+a=pela, até+a=até à.

exercises.title: Choose the Correct Movement Preposition
exercises.instructions: Select the correct preposition for each sentence.

items.question: Vou ___ supermercado e volto.
items.explanation: Short trip (you'll return): 'ao supermercado'. A + o = ao.

items.question: O comboio vai ___ Porto.
items.explanation: Destination: 'para o Porto' = to Porto (the train's destination).

items.question: Vou ___ praça e depois volto.
items.explanation: 'Até' sets a limit: 'até à praça' = as far as the square (and no further).

items.question: Passa ___ centro da cidade.
items.explanation: 'Por' = through: 'pelo centro' = through the center. Por + o = pelo.

exercises.title: Give Directions
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank with the correct direction expression.

items.sentence: ___ em frente até ao semáforo. (go straight)

items.hint: Formal imperative of passar (to pass through)

exercises.title: Ask for Directions
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct question for each situation.

items.question: You want to know where the station is:
options: Como se vai para a estação?
items.explanation: Both 'Onde é a estação?' and 'Como se vai para a estação?' are correct ways to ask where the station is.

items.question: You want to know how to get to the city center:
items.explanation: 'Como chego ao centro?' = How do I get to the center? This asks for directions, not just location.

exercises.title: Give Directions
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct preposition to complete each direction.

items.hint: Means of transport: de + metro = by metro
As far as: até + a = até à
